source: Willcox, G. (1982), The Feeling Wheel. Secondary words follow the Willcox/Roberts lineage; tertiary words curated for ND Toolbox.

nodes.label: Joyful
nodes.definition: A bright, warm feeling of gladness or delight.

guidance.pointsTo: Joy often shows up when something matters to you and is going well.
guidance.oneOption: If it fits, you might pause a moment to let the good feeling land.

nodes.label: Content
nodes.definition: Quietly satisfied; you have enough right now.

guidance.pointsTo: Contentment often points to a need that is, for now, met.
guidance.oneOption: You might simply notice what is going right, no need to change anything.

nodes.label: Satisfied
nodes.definition: Pleased with how something turned out.

guidance.pointsTo: Satisfaction often points to something that matched what you hoped for.
guidance.oneOption: You might let yourself register that it worked out, before moving to the next thing.

nodes.label: Fulfilled
nodes.definition: Full and complete, as if something meaningful is done.

guidance.pointsTo: Fulfilment often points to something that lined up with what matters to you.
guidance.oneOption: You might note what made it meaningful, so you can seek more of it.

guidance.pointsTo: Happiness often follows a moment that fits what you value.
guidance.oneOption: You might name out loud one thing that sparked it.

guidance.pointsTo: Cheerfulness often shows up when nothing is weighing on you just now.
guidance.oneOption: You might let it be light, with no need to justify it.

nodes.label: Delighted
nodes.definition: Sharply, happily surprised by something good.

guidance.pointsTo: Delight often points to a good surprise — something better than you expected.
guidance.oneOption: You might share it with someone; delight tends to grow when it is passed on.

nodes.label: Glad
nodes.definition: Simply pleased that something is so.

guidance.pointsTo: Gladness often points to a small thing quietly going right.
guidance.oneOption: You might name the one thing you are glad of, out loud or to yourself.

nodes.label: Excited
nodes.definition: Lit up and energized about what is ahead.

guidance.pointsTo: Excitement often points to something you are looking forward to.
guidance.oneOption: You might channel a little of the energy into one small first step.

guidance.pointsTo: Energy often points to a body and mind that are ready to move.
guidance.oneOption: You might spend a little of it on something you actually want to do.

guidance.pointsTo: Eagerness often points to something ahead that you care about.
guidance.oneOption: You might channel it into one small first step now.

guidance.pointsTo: Playfulness often shows up when you feel safe enough to be loose.
guidance.oneOption: You might give yourself permission to follow it for a few minutes.

nodes.label: Amused
nodes.definition: Quietly entertained; something struck you as funny.

guidance.pointsTo: Amusement often shows up when something catches you pleasantly off guard.
guidance.oneOption: You might let yourself enjoy it without analysing why it is funny.

nodes.label: Lighthearted
nodes.definition: Free of weight or worry for now.

guidance.pointsTo: Lightheartedness often shows up when, for now, nothing needs carrying.
guidance.oneOption: You might let this be a break, and pick the weight back up later.

guidance.pointsTo: Optimism often points to a sense that effort here is worth it.
guidance.oneOption: You might jot one hope so you can return to it later.

guidance.pointsTo: Hope often points to a sense that things could genuinely go well.
guidance.oneOption: You might write the hope down so you can return to it on a harder day.

nodes.label: Inspired
nodes.definition: Moved to make or do something.

guidance.pointsTo: Inspiration often points to something you would like to make or do.
guidance.oneOption: You might capture the idea quickly, before it fades.

nodes.label: Interested
nodes.definition: Drawn in; wanting to know more.

guidance.pointsTo: Interest often points to something worth your attention.
guidance.oneOption: You might follow the curiosity one question further.

guidance.pointsTo: Curiosity often points to something worth a closer look.
guidance.oneOption: You might follow it one question further.

guidance.pointsTo: Absorption often shows up when something fits your attention just right.
guidance.oneOption: You might protect the focus for a while longer, if you can.

guidance.pointsTo: Engagement often points to something holding your interest and effort.
guidance.oneOption: You might notice what makes it click, so you can find more of it.

nodes.label: Powerful
nodes.definition: A feeling of capability, confidence, and agency.

guidance.pointsTo: A sense of power often points to something you can affect or do.
guidance.oneOption: If it fits, you might name the one thing that is yours to act on.

nodes.label: Confident
nodes.definition: Sure of yourself and what you can do.

guidance.pointsTo: Confidence often points to skill or preparation you can trust.
guidance.oneOption: You might let one recent success stand as evidence.

guidance.pointsTo: Self-assurance often points to a settled sense of who you are.
guidance.oneOption: You might let it steady you, without needing to prove it to anyone.

guidance.pointsTo: Boldness often points to caring about something more than the risk of it.
guidance.oneOption: You might take the one brave step while the nerve is here.

nodes.label: Empowered
nodes.definition: Free and able to act on your own terms.

guidance.pointsTo: Feeling empowered often points to a real choice being yours to make.
guidance.oneOption: You might name the one thing that is genuinely in your hands.

nodes.label: Proud
nodes.definition: Warmly pleased with something you did or are.

guidance.pointsTo: Pride often points to effort or values you lived up to.
guidance.oneOption: You might let yourself take quiet credit for it.

nodes.label: Accomplished
nodes.definition: You achieved something that took work.

guidance.pointsTo: Accomplishment often points to effort that finally paid off.
guidance.oneOption: You might take quiet credit before rushing to the next task.

guidance.pointsTo: Feeling worthy often points to a sense that you matter as you are.
guidance.oneOption: You might let that be true without earning it today.

guidance.pointsTo: Triumph often points to something hard that you came through.
guidance.oneOption: You might mark it somehow, even quietly, so it counts.

guidance.pointsTo: Feeling respected often points to a need to be taken seriously being met.
guidance.oneOption: You might notice who offered it, and let it register.

guidance.pointsTo: Feeling valued often points to being treated as if you matter here.
guidance.oneOption: You might let it land, rather than waving it off.

nodes.label: Admired
nodes.definition: Looked up to for who you are or what you do.

guidance.pointsTo: Being admired often points to something real in you that others can see.
guidance.oneOption: You might consider believing them, just a little.

nodes.label: Capable
nodes.definition: Up to the task in front of you.

guidance.pointsTo: Capability often points to a match between the task and your skills.
guidance.oneOption: You might start with the part you already know how to do.

guidance.pointsTo: Competence often points to skill you have built and can trust.
guidance.oneOption: You might start with the part you already know how to do.

nodes.label: Resourceful
nodes.definition: Good at finding a way through.

guidance.pointsTo: Resourcefulness often points to your knack for finding a way through.
guidance.oneOption: You might trust that you will figure out the next bit too.

guidance.pointsTo: Determination often points to a goal that matters to you.
guidance.oneOption: You might define the next single step and stop there.

guidance.pointsTo: Motivation often points to a goal that feels worth the effort.
guidance.oneOption: You might define the single next step and stop there.

guidance.pointsTo: Focus often shows up when your attention and the task line up.
guidance.oneOption: You might guard the quiet around it while it lasts.

guidance.pointsTo: Feeling appreciated often points to a need to be seen being met.
guidance.oneOption: You might let the acknowledgment fully arrive before moving on.

guidance.pointsTo: Being recognized often points to a need to be seen being met.
guidance.oneOption: You might let the acknowledgement fully arrive.

nodes.label: Important
nodes.definition: Your presence makes a difference here.

guidance.pointsTo: Feeling important often points to your presence making a real difference here.
guidance.oneOption: You might notice where you are most needed, and where you are free to rest.

guidance.pointsTo: Peace often shows up when nothing urgent is pulling at you.
guidance.oneOption: If it fits, you might slow one breath and stay here a beat longer.

guidance.pointsTo: Calm often points to a nervous system that feels safe enough.
guidance.oneOption: You might let your shoulders drop and notice the quiet.

guidance.pointsTo: Feeling settled often points to a body that feels safe enough to rest.
guidance.oneOption: You might let your shoulders drop and stay a moment longer.

guidance.pointsTo: Stillness often shows up when nothing is pulling at you.
guidance.oneOption: You might simply notice the quiet, without needing to fill it.

guidance.pointsTo: Relaxation often points to a load that has, for now, lifted.
guidance.oneOption: You might let one part of your body soften a little more.

guidance.pointsTo: Ease often points to a load that has, for now, lifted.
guidance.oneOption: You might let one more part of you soften.

guidance.pointsTo: Feeling unhurried often points to enough time, and permission to use it.
guidance.oneOption: You might take the next thing slowly, on purpose.

guidance.pointsTo: Security often points to a need for safety being met right now.
guidance.oneOption: You might notice one thing that is holding you steady.

guidance.pointsTo: Safety often points to a need for protection being met right now.
guidance.oneOption: You might notice the one thing helping you feel it.

nodes.label: Grounded
nodes.definition: Rooted and present in your body.

guidance.pointsTo: Feeling grounded often points to being present in your body and the moment.
guidance.oneOption: You might feel your feet on the floor and stay there a breath.

nodes.label: Protected
nodes.definition: Shielded from what could harm you.

guidance.pointsTo: Feeling protected often points to something or someone keeping you safe.
guidance.oneOption: You might let yourself lean on it a little.

guidance.pointsTo: Love often points to a connection that matters to you.
guidance.oneOption: You might let the person know, in a small way, if it feels right.

guidance.pointsTo: Affection often points to warmth toward someone who matters.
guidance.oneOption: You might let them know, in a small way, if it feels right.

guidance.pointsTo: Tenderness often points to something soft and open in you right now.
guidance.oneOption: You might treat both yourself and them gently while it is here.

guidance.pointsTo: Devotion often points to a care you have chosen and want to keep.
guidance.oneOption: You might notice what makes it worth it to you.

nodes.label: Grateful
nodes.definition: Thankful for something you have or received.

guidance.pointsTo: Gratitude often points to something good you have noticed.
guidance.oneOption: You might name one thing, however small, that you are glad of.

guidance.pointsTo: Thankfulness often points to a specific good you have noticed.
guidance.oneOption: You might name the one thing, however small.

nodes.label: Warm
nodes.definition: Softly glowing toward someone or something.

guidance.pointsTo: Warmth often points to a quiet glow toward someone or something.
guidance.oneOption: You might stay with the feeling a moment before it passes.

nodes.label: Trusting
nodes.definition: Willing to be open and rely on someone.

guidance.pointsTo: Trust often points to a bond that has felt safe over time.
guidance.oneOption: You might let one small honesty pass between you.

guidance.pointsTo: Openness often points to feeling safe enough to receive and share.
guidance.oneOption: You might let one honest thing pass between you.

guidance.pointsTo: Connection often points to genuine contact with someone.
guidance.oneOption: You might do one small thing to keep the thread alive.

nodes.label: Sad
nodes.definition: A heavy, low feeling, often tied to loss.

guidance.pointsTo: Sadness often points to something you care about that has been lost or missed.
guidance.oneOption: If it fits, you might let the feeling be here without needing to fix it.

guidance.pointsTo: Loneliness often points to a need for connection that is not being met right now.
guidance.oneOption: You might send one small message to someone you trust — no pressure to.

guidance.pointsTo: Isolation often points to a need for connection that is not being met right now.
guidance.oneOption: You might make one small reach toward someone — there is no pressure to.

nodes.label: Abandoned
nodes.definition: Left behind by someone you counted on.

guidance.pointsTo: Feeling abandoned often points to counting on someone who was not there.
guidance.oneOption: You might be extra gentle with yourself; this one cuts deep.

nodes.label: Disconnected
nodes.definition: Present with others but not in contact.

guidance.pointsTo: Disconnection often points to being near others but not quite in contact.
guidance.oneOption: You might name what would help you feel met, even if you cannot act on it yet.

guidance.pointsTo: Hurt often points to something that mattered being damaged or dismissed.
guidance.oneOption: You might place a hand where you feel it and breathe once, slowly.

nodes.label: Wounded
nodes.definition: Cut deeply by words or actions.

guidance.pointsTo: Feeling wounded often points to something that mattered being hurt or dismissed.
guidance.oneOption: You might place a hand where you feel it and breathe once, slowly.

nodes.label: Heartbroken
nodes.definition: In deep grief over a loss.

guidance.pointsTo: Heartbreak often points to the loss of something you loved — its size matches how much it meant.
guidance.oneOption: You might let the grief be here without rushing it toward okay.

nodes.label: Disappointed
nodes.definition: Let down when things fell short.

guidance.pointsTo: Disappointment often points to a hope that did not come to pass.
guidance.oneOption: You might name what you had wanted, plainly, to yourself.

guidance.pointsTo: Feeling let down often points to a hope or expectation that was not met.
guidance.oneOption: You might name plainly what you had wanted, just to yourself.

guidance.pointsTo: Discouragement often points to effort that has not paid off yet.
guidance.oneOption: You might rest before deciding whether to keep going.

nodes.label: Depressed
nodes.definition: Heavily low, often flat or empty.

guidance.pointsTo: This heaviness often points to being depleted or grieving, not to a flaw in you.
guidance.oneOption: You might make the next step very small — a glass of water counts.

nodes.label: Empty
nodes.definition: Hollow, as if something is missing.

guidance.pointsTo: Emptiness often points to being depleted, or to something missing — not to a flaw in you.
guidance.oneOption: You might make the next step very small; a glass of water counts.

guidance.pointsTo: Hopelessness often settles in when you are worn down, and it tends to lift more than it feels like it will right now.
guidance.oneOption: You might hold off on big conclusions until you have rested or eaten.

nodes.label: Numb
nodes.definition: Feeling little or nothing at all.

guidance.pointsTo: Numbness is often a way the mind protects you when things are too much — not a sign that nothing matters.
guidance.oneOption: You might let yourself feel little for now; sensation tends to return.

nodes.label: Guilty
nodes.definition: Uneasy that you did something wrong.

guidance.pointsTo: Guilt often points to a value of yours that you feel you crossed.
guidance.oneOption: You might ask what, if anything, is actually yours to repair.

nodes.label: Ashamed
nodes.definition: Wanting to hide, as if you are bad.

guidance.pointsTo: Shame often says you ARE bad, when at most you did something you regret — those are not the same.
guidance.oneOption: You might name the specific thing, which is smaller than the whole-self feeling.

nodes.label: Remorseful
nodes.definition: Sorry and wishing you had acted differently.

guidance.pointsTo: Remorse often points to a value of yours you feel you crossed.
guidance.oneOption: You might ask what, if anything, is actually yours to repair.

nodes.label: Vulnerable
nodes.definition: Exposed and open to being hurt.

guidance.pointsTo: Vulnerability often points to something tender being close to the surface.
guidance.oneOption: You might choose who, if anyone, is safe enough to see it.

guidance.pointsTo: Fragility often points to being close to your limit right now.
guidance.oneOption: You might lower what you ask of yourself for a while.

guidance.pointsTo: Feeling exposed often points to something tender being uncomfortably seen.
guidance.oneOption: You might choose who, if anyone, is safe enough to see it.

nodes.label: Mad
nodes.definition: A charged feeling that something is wrong or unfair.

guidance.pointsTo: Anger often points to a boundary crossed or a need going unmet.
guidance.oneOption: If it fits, you might ask what the anger is trying to protect.

guidance.pointsTo: Frustration often points to an obstacle between you and a goal.
guidance.oneOption: You might name the specific blocker; it is often smaller once named.

guidance.pointsTo: Feeling blocked often points to an obstacle between you and something you want.
guidance.oneOption: You might name the specific blocker; it is often smaller once named.

guidance.pointsTo: Exasperation often points to the same trouble happening one time too many.
guidance.oneOption: You might step back before responding to the latest round.

nodes.label: Fed up
nodes.definition: Done with putting up with it.

guidance.pointsTo: Being fed up often points to a limit you have quietly reached.
guidance.oneOption: You might ask what you are no longer willing to put up with.

guidance.pointsTo: Irritation often points to a small, repeated friction — or to being depleted.
guidance.oneOption: You might check whether you are hungry, tired, or overstimulated first.

guidance.pointsTo: Annoyance often points to a small friction — or to being tired or hungry underneath.
guidance.oneOption: You might check whether it is really the thing, or the state you are in.

guidance.pointsTo: Agitation often points to stirred-up energy with nowhere to go.
guidance.oneOption: You might give it an outlet — move, or step outside for a minute.

guidance.pointsTo: Resentment often points to a hurt that was never fully addressed.
guidance.oneOption: You might name the unmet expectation underneath it.

guidance.pointsTo: Bitterness often points to a hurt that never got fully addressed.
guidance.oneOption: You might name the unmet expectation underneath it.

guidance.pointsTo: Indignation often points to something you see as clearly unfair.
guidance.oneOption: You might separate what is yours to act on from what is not.

nodes.label: Hostile
nodes.definition: Strongly angry, ready to push back.

guidance.pointsTo: Hostility often points to feeling attacked or deeply wronged.
guidance.oneOption: You might give the heat somewhere safe to go — move, or step away.

guidance.pointsTo: Fury often points to a boundary or value that feels badly crossed.
guidance.oneOption: You might give the heat somewhere safe to go before you act on it.

guidance.pointsTo: Rage often points to something that feels deeply wrong or threatening.
guidance.oneOption: You might move your body or step away until the peak passes.

nodes.label: Seething
nodes.definition: Anger held tightly under the surface.

guidance.pointsTo: Seething often points to anger held tightly under the surface.
guidance.oneOption: You might let it out somewhere safe, rather than only holding it in.

nodes.label: Jealous
nodes.definition: Threatened by what someone else has.

guidance.pointsTo: Jealousy often points to something you want or fear losing.
guidance.oneOption: You might name what you actually want underneath the comparison.

guidance.pointsTo: Envy often points to something you want, shown to you by someone who has it.
guidance.oneOption: You might name what you actually want underneath the comparison.

nodes.label: Possessive
nodes.definition: Afraid of losing someone or something.

guidance.pointsTo: Possessiveness often points to a fear of losing someone or something.
guidance.oneOption: You might name the fear directly; it is usually softer than the grip.

nodes.label: Disrespected
nodes.definition: Treated as if you do not matter.

guidance.pointsTo: Feeling disrespected often points to a need to be valued being crossed.
guidance.oneOption: You might decide what boundary, if any, you want to name later.

guidance.pointsTo: Feeling dismissed often points to a need to be taken seriously being crossed.
guidance.oneOption: You might decide what, if anything, you want to name later.

guidance.pointsTo: Feeling belittled often points to being made smaller than you are.
guidance.oneOption: You might remind yourself of the size you actually take up.

nodes.label: Scared
nodes.definition: A guarded feeling of threat or uncertainty.

guidance.pointsTo: Fear often points to something that feels unsafe or unknown.
guidance.oneOption: If it fits, you might ask whether the danger is here now, or anticipated.

nodes.label: Anxious
nodes.definition: Keyed up and uneasy about what might happen.

guidance.pointsTo: Anxiety often points to uncertainty your mind is trying to solve ahead of time.
guidance.oneOption: You might bring attention to one thing you can sense right now.

guidance.pointsTo: Nervousness often points to something ahead that matters to you.
guidance.oneOption: You might bring your attention to one thing you can sense right now.

nodes.label: Worried
nodes.definition: Turning a concern over and over.

guidance.pointsTo: Worry often points to a concern your mind is trying to solve ahead of time.
guidance.oneOption: You might write the worry down so it can stop circling.

nodes.label: Uneasy
nodes.definition: Something feels off, hard to name.

guidance.pointsTo: Unease often points to something feeling off before you can name it.
guidance.oneOption: You might trust the signal and give it time to become clearer.

nodes.label: Insecure
nodes.definition: Unsure of your footing or worth.

guidance.pointsTo: Insecurity often points to a fear of not being enough in this moment.
guidance.oneOption: You might recall one time you handled something like this.

guidance.pointsTo: Feeling inadequate often points to a fear of not being enough in this moment.
guidance.oneOption: You might recall one time you handled something like this.

nodes.label: Self-conscious
nodes.definition: Overly aware of being watched or judged.

guidance.pointsTo: Self-consciousness often points to feeling watched or judged.
guidance.oneOption: You might notice that most people are far more focused on themselves.

guidance.pointsTo: Overwhelm often points to more input or demand than capacity right now.
guidance.oneOption: You might reduce the field to the single next action, and hide the rest.

guidance.pointsTo: Stress often points to more demand than capacity right now.
guidance.oneOption: You might reduce the field to the single next action and hide the rest.

guidance.pointsTo: Feeling frazzled often points to too many threads pulled at once.
guidance.oneOption: You might drop all but one thread for a few minutes.

nodes.label: Swamped
nodes.definition: Buried under too much to do.

guidance.pointsTo: Feeling swamped often points to more to do than any one person could hold.
guidance.oneOption: You might pick the one thing that would ease the most, and start there.

guidance.pointsTo: Rejection often points to a need to belong that feels threatened.
guidance.oneOption: You might remind yourself that one no is not a verdict on your worth.

guidance.pointsTo: Feeling excluded often points to a need to belong that feels threatened.
guidance.oneOption: You might remind yourself that one room is not everywhere.

nodes.label: Unwanted
nodes.definition: As if your presence is not welcome.

guidance.pointsTo: Feeling unwanted often points to a fear that your presence is not welcome.
guidance.oneOption: You might be gentle with yourself, and check the story against the facts later.

nodes.label: Confused
nodes.definition: Unable to make sense of things.

guidance.pointsTo: Confusion often points to missing information or mixed signals.
guidance.oneOption: You might write down the one question you most want answered.

guidance.pointsTo: Bewilderment often points to missing information or mixed signals.
guidance.oneOption: You might write down the one question you most want answered.

guidance.pointsTo: Uncertainty often points to not yet having enough to choose.
guidance.oneOption: You might name what you would need to know to feel surer.

guidance.pointsTo: Feeling threatened often points to a real or perceived danger to you or what you hold.
guidance.oneOption: You might create a little distance or a barrier while you assess.

guidance.pointsTo: Alarm often points to something sudden your body flagged as a threat.
guidance.oneOption: You might take one slow breath and check whether the danger is here now.

guidance.pointsTo: Defensiveness often points to feeling got at, and bracing to protect yourself.
guidance.oneOption: You might pause to check whether you are actually under attack right now.
